description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a dark brown to gray coloration. One prominent face features a red petroglyph-style drawing resembling an animal, which stands out against the natural stone surface. The stone has a rough, fractured texture with visible chips and jagged edges, suggesting it has been broken or weathered over time. The base is surrounded by smaller stone fragments, indicating it may have split from a larger rock or been placed intentionally. The surface is mostly matte and uneven, with some areas appearing slightly lighter due to natural weathering. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban park or archaeological-themed public space.
description: A large, jagged stone with a dark, rough surface and a red petroglyph-style animal drawing on one side, surrounded by smaller rock fragments.
description_view_0: The main face displays a red animal petroglyph on a dark, rough surface with jagged lower edges.
description_view_1: Tall, angular side with deep creases and a more vertical, monolithic appearance; no visible markings.
description_view_2: Broad, pale surface with a rough, granular texture and several attached stone fragments at the base.
description_view_3: Narrow, elongated view showing the stone's height and a mix of light and dark surfaces, with no visible markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Features a red petroglyph-style animal drawing on one face; otherwise, the surface is irregular with natural fractures and no regular pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and fractured with visible chips and some lighter weathered areas, indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban environments but could blend with naturalistic landscaping or archaeological displays; surrounded by smaller stone fragments.
